15.reason: [If the file fails to open, rstream is NULL and fgetc(NULL) causes undefined behavior (likely crash).]

17.reason: [No check for malloc failure; if allocation fails, q is NULL and subsequent accesses cause crashes.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [No check for malloc failure; if allocation fails, q is NULL and subsequent accesses cause crashes.]

19.reason: [This loop condition does not handle non-alphabetic characters correctly per problem definition; it treats any non-space/non-newline as part of a word, including punctuation and digits. Also, if c is EOF at start, loop condition may still pass incorrectly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This loop condition does not handle non-alphabetic characters correctly per problem definition; it treats any non-space/non-newline as part of a word, including punctuation and digits. Also, if c is EOF at start, loop condition may still pass incorrectly.]

26.reason: [This decrements i to "undo" non-letter, but doesn't skip the character properly; also, if multiple non-letters occur, this logic breaks. Moreover, buffer overflow possible if too many letters.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This decrements i to "undo" non-letter, but doesn't skip the character properly; also, if multiple non-letters occur, this logic breaks. Moreover, buffer overflow possible if too many letters.]

63.reason: [This loop assumes list and r form a proper linked list with r being the tail, but r is only updated in one branch; in other cases, r may be stale or uninitialized, leading to infinite loop or crash. Also, d1->p may be NULL inside loop, causing strcmp(q->s,d1->p->s) to dereference NULL.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This loop assumes list and r form a proper linked list with r being the tail, but r is only updated in one branch; in other cases, r may be stale or uninitialized, leading to infinite loop or crash. Also, d1->p may be NULL inside loop, causing strcmp(q->s,d1->p->s) to dereference NULL.]

66.reason: [d1->p could be NULL here, leading to segmentation fault when accessing d1->p->s.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [d1->p could be NULL here, leading to segmentation fault when accessing d1->p->s.]

77.reason: [After inner loop reads until space/newline, this reads next char, but skips processing of words that end at EOF without trailing space/newline. Also, if file ends right after a word, last word might be missed or processed incorrectly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [After inner loop reads until space/newline, this reads next char, but skips processing of words that end at EOF without trailing space/newline. Also, if file ends right after a word, last word might be missed or processed incorrectly.]
